channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court Stays Pawan Khera's Anticipatory Bail in Assam Passport Allegations Case
item.description: The Supreme Court has intervened in the ongoing legal battle between Congress leader Pawan Khera and the Assam government, staying the Telangana High Court's order granting him one-week transit antici...

item.title: Access Issue with AP Inter 1st and 2nd Year Result 2026 Online
item.description: The Andhra Pradesh Intermediate (AP Inter) 1st and 2nd Year Result for 2026 was supposed to be accessible through the official NDTV education portal. Students and parents were eagerly awaiting live up...

item.title: Global markets inch higher as optimism grows over potential U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks
item.description: Global stock markets moved closer to record highs on Wednesday, supported by news that the United States and Iran may resume diplomatic talks within the next two days. The MSCI All-Country World Index...

item.title: PM Shehbaz begins three-nation diplomatic tour to Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has left for Saudi Arabia on April 15, marking the start of his three-nation official tour which will continue until April 18. Along with Saudi Arabia, PM Shehbaz will vi...

item.title: Arsenal Face Title Pressure but Youngster Max Dowman Offers Hope Ahead of Sporting Clash
item.description: Arsenal are navigating a turbulent period in their season, with recent setbacks threatening their Premier League title aspirations. Mikel Arteta's side sit six points clear with six matches remaining,...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal gardener wanted for stalking after job dismissal
item.description: A KwaZulu-Natal woman from eThekwini is living in fear after her former gardener allegedly began stalking her following the rejection of his romantic advances. The gardener, a foreign national employe...

item.title: Former Defence Ministers Urge Welfare Cuts to Fund UK's Security Needs
item.description: Two former Labour defence secretaries, Lord Robertson and Lord Hutton, have publicly criticised the Government for failing to prioritise defence spending, highlighting that Britain's welfare budget no...

item.title: Schmaltz leads Mammoth to 5-3 victory over Jets
item.description: In a National Lacrosse League matchup in Winnipeg, Ryan Schmaltz delivered an outstanding performance, scoring two goals to help the Mammoth secure a 5-3 win over the Jets. The game showcased strong t...

item.title: Kenyan Trucker Dies in DRC Following Breakdown and Military Abandonment
item.description: A heartbreaking situation unfolded in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where Edwin Njunguna Ngugi, a Kenyan long-distance truck driver, met a tragic end. Ngugi, who was transporting goods from Mombas...

item.title: Senate probes unregulated business dealings by inmates in Philippine jails
item.description: A Senate committee raised concern over alleged unregulated business operations happening inside Philippine detention facilities. Senator Raffy Tulfo cited a case from Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu, involving a...

item.title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah emphasises INDIA bloc’s collective stance on Women’s Reservation Bill and delimitation
item.description: On Wednesday, Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah stated that the INDIA bloc will decide collectively on their response to the Women's Reservation Bill and concerns about delimitation durin...

item.title: Aurangzeb Discusses Economic Cooperation and IMF Programme During Washington Visit
item.description: Pakistan’s Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb has held key meetings in Washington during the IMF and World Bank Spring Meetings 2026 to reinforce Pakistan’s economic strategy amid ongoing global chal...

item.title: International Photo Highlights Selected by The Age Editors
item.description: The article titled 'World in Pictures, April 15, 2026' published by The Age showcases a curated gallery of eight compelling images from leading international wire agencies. These photographs, chosen b...

item.title: President Marcos Accepts Eduardo Año’s Resignation; Eduardo Oban Named New National Security Adviser
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. has officially accepted the resignation of Eduardo Año from his post as National Security Adviser (NSA), according to Malacañang. This was confirmed by Palace...

item.title: Leeds plans for trams receive government backing but delays remain
item.description: Leeds has long aspired to reintroduce trams to the city, a goal dating back to abandoned plans in the 1980s and early 1990s. Previous schemes, including the Supertram and a trolleybus network, were ca...

item.title: Sam Kerr Warns Harambee Starlets Ahead of FIFA Series Final Against Australia
item.description: Kenya's Harambee Starlets are set for a tough challenge in the FIFA Women's Series final at Nyayo National Stadium, Nairobi, where they will face Australia, the 15th-ranked team globally. Australia's ...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Criticizes Trump Over Pardons During Late-Night Monologue
item.description: On April 15, 2026, late-night host Jimmy Kimmel addressed President Donald Trump's recent actions during his monologue, focusing on his extensive use of pardons. Kimmel humorously suggested that Trump...

item.title: Indian man charged in Australia for alleged sexual assault on Singapore–Perth flight
item.description: An Indian citizen, aged 52, has been charged in Australia for allegedly assaulting a woman during a flight from Singapore to Perth. According to the Australian Federal Police (AFP) and Western Austral...

item.title: How the ‘husband stitch’ in Nigerian hospitals is harming women after childbirth
item.description: This investigation by DUBAWA for Premium Times exposes how some Nigerian health workers perform an extra ‘husband stitch’ after childbirth to make women’s vagina tighter for their husbands’ sexual sat...

item.title: Japanese researcher ensures continuation of 1,200-year cherry blossom data after Prof Yasuyuki Aono’s death
item.description: Prof Yasuyuki Aono of Osaka Metropolitan University dedicated his life to studying Japan’s cherry blossom flowering dates, creating one of the world’s longest continuous climate records spanning more ...
